summary: iter254 — Auto-ban (automatic blocking of attackers) feature E2E verified. Backend 7/7 PASS (test_autoban_iter254.py). Frontend regression PASS — the new axios response interceptor in AuthContext.js does NOT break normal app load, admin login succeeds with dashboard rendering, wrong login still shows normal error (not block page), and Super Admin Security Log correctly renders auto-banned entries with 'تلقائي' badge + reason text referencing the attempted path.
critical_code_review_comments: _register_offense reads audit_logs WITHOUT a tenant filter to find 'login.success' — if any tenant ever has a successful login from the same outbound NAT IP, the attacker hiding behind that same NAT will be whitelisted forever. For carrier/corp NATs in production this could let attackers bypass auto-ban. Recommend: scope safeguard to recent login.success only (last 24h) and require at least 1 non-suspicious request from the IP after the login (or per-tenant IP whitelist).

_AUTO_OFFENSES is an in-process defaultdict keyed by IP. With multiple uvicorn workers/pods, each replica counts offenses independently — an attacker hitting N replicas needs ~N×20 offenses to be banned. Move counter to Redis or Mongo with TTL once horizontally scaled.

_client_ip uses request.headers['x-forwarded-for'].split(',')[0] — that is the LEFT-MOST entry which (under standard nginx) is the original client. Good. But the code path is reachable via direct curl from outside the ingress with a fabricated XFF header. Verify ingress strips/replaces XFF in production; if it merely appends, any attacker can spoof a different victim IP to be auto-banned (creates a denial-of-service for that victim once they connect legitimately). Recommend trust_proxy_count style validation: take the Nth-from-right entry equal to the known ingress hops, not split[0].

The block HTML page in _block_response is rendered server-side as inline HTML string; the same HTML is also re-rendered client-side from AuthContext.js. Two sources of truth — if branding/text changes one is missed. Consider serving a static /blocked.html and redirecting / referencing from both sides.

axios response interceptor in AuthContext.js writes directly to document.body.innerHTML (replaces ALL React DOM). Once shown there is no way to recover the SPA without a full reload — fine for an attacker-block UX, but if a server bug ever sends {blocked:true} accidentally on a legitimate 403, the user will be stuck. Recommend defending with a server-side cookie or explicit 'X-Blocked: 1' header AND data.blocked === true (two signals), to make false-positives impossible.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin login OK, dashboard renders, wrong login NOT blocked, super-admin security log shows auto entries with badge + reason + manual/unblock buttons)

test_credentials: Admin admin@maestroegp.com/admin123 ; Super Admin owner@maestroegp.com/owner123 + secret 271018
seed_data_creation: Temporarily auto-banned IPs 198.51.100.42 (via test fixture) and 203.0.113.99 (via UI test, spoofed XFF). Both unblocked at end of run. Final blocked-ips list = []. No persistent seed remains.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_autoban_iter254.py -v` to regression-check auto-ban. Tests cover: 20+ forbidden POST /api/products with spoofed X-Forwarded-For trigger auto-add to blocked_ips (auto=true, last_path=/api/products), banned XFF gets 403 + JSON {blocked:true}, pod's real IP stays 200 (safeguard via login.success audit), unblock removes entry + XFF can reach API again, admin login regression OK, wrong-password login returns 401 NOT 403/blocked (interceptor doesn't false-fire), and blocked-ips listing exposes auto entries with reason. FRONTEND: SuperAdmin → tab data-testid='security-log-tab' → blocked-ips-section renders with 'تلقائي' badge and reason text referencing attempted path. NOTE: ingress is happy to receive an X-Forwarded-For from the test pod and use the leftmost entry — this is OK for testing but flagged in code review as a hardening item.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature works as designed. Auto-ban triggers on 20 rejected writes within 5 min, safeguard skips IPs with a prior login.success, blocked IPs hit the security_headers_middleware and get _block_response (HTML for browsers, JSON for API). Frontend axios response interceptor only activates when status===403 AND data.blocked===true, so normal 401/403 errors (e.g., wrong password, permission denied) flow through unchanged — verified end-to-end in the regression playwright run.
